pattern_recognition: Binary Search, as the problem involves searching in a sorted array with a twist (rotation). The presence of two sorted halves indicates the potential for a logarithmic search approach.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Simply iterate through the entire array to find the minimum element.
approaches.algorithm: Loop through each element and keep track of the minimum found.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Utilize binary search to efficiently find the minimum element by narrowing down the search space based on the properties of the rotated array.
approaches.algorithm: Use two pointers to represent the search range and adjust them based on the mid-point comparison.

dry_run: Consider the input array [4, 5, 6, 7, 0, 1, 2]. Start with left at index 0 and right at index 6. Calculate mid as (0 + 6) / 2 = 3. The value at mid is 7. Since 7 > 0 (value at right), we know the minimum must be in the right half. Move left to mid + 1. Repeat until left equals right, which will point to the minimum element.
edge_cases: An empty input should return an indication of no minimum. A single element array should return that element as the minimum. Since the problem specifies unique elements, duplicates are not a concern. Large inputs should be handled efficiently by the optimal approach without performance degradation.
common_pitfalls: 1) Not recognizing that the array is rotated and treating it as a standard sorted array. 2) Failing to adjust the search range correctly based on the mid-point value. 3) Confusing the conditions for moving the left and right pointers, leading to infinite loops or incorrect results.
